mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 higher over a quarterly horizon as investors price in stronger fundamentals.
failure_condition: If over any rolling 5-year period the correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not positive and statistically significant (p>0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, it signals that monetary policy is restrictive, increasing the discount rate on future earnings and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If forward 20-day SPX returns after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% are positive in more than 40% of occurrences over any 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between industrial production growth and retail sales growth indicates inventory accumulation relative to final demand, foreshadowing future production cuts and earnings declines that depress equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the spread between INDPRO and RSAFS 3-month growth rates shows zero or positive correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, sparking recession fears that cause immediate negative S&P 500 returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: If the mean 5-day SPX return following UNRATE increases >0.2pp is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05) over all occurrences, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a high inflation regime (CPI year-over-year above 3%), a rising federal funds rate amplifies tightening fears and compounds the negative impact on equity valuations, producing stronger negative forward returns than in low inflation regimes.
failure_condition: If within the high inflation regime the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward SPX returns is not negative and significant at 95% confidence over any 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in housing starts reflects strong housing demand and broader economic confidence, which leads to higher corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST 6-month growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales growth exceeds 0.5% and the federal funds rate is below 3%, the economy exhibits strong demand with accommodative monetary policy, creating a favorable environment for equities and generating positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If the mean 20-day SPX return when both conditions hold is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05) over all instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal the onset of a manufacturing recession, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If forward 60-day SPX returns after the trigger condition are positive in more than 40% of cases over any 5-year period, reject.
